title: Evangelion 10 You Are Not Alone in Washington DC
display_title: Evangelion 10 You Are Not Alone in Washington DC
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone in Washington, DC             Quote Post by JeffersonLeeEng » Tue Mar 03, 2009 5:01 pm It should be noted that there will be a screening of Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone in Washington, DC on March 28th at 1:30pm. This is part of the Seventh Annual National Cherry Blossom Festival Anime Marathon at the Freer Gallery of Art [ 1050 Independence Ave SW Washington, DC 20439 - Telephone: 202.633.1000] Evangelion 1.0: You Are (Not) Alone 1:30 PM This sci-fi tale co-directed by Hideaki Anno, Masayuki and Kazuya Tsurumaki is set in a post-apocalyptic Tokyo, where the population defends itself from alien invaders with the help of human/mechanical hybrid battle robots. Its hero, Shinji, is a shy 14-year-old boy mysteriously chosen to save the planet from the aliens for good. Rated PG. (2007, 98 min., Japanese with English subtitles, video)

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Mar 03, 2009 8:04 pm Oh D.C. Driving distance! I was so excited about this until I looked at the site. It says Video. Which leads me to believe it's really just a DVD projected big. Not film. Still...it would be Eva 1.0 in a theater like environment...I'll have to think about this one. PS. Interesting to note Eva 1.0 is apparently a PG film. Makes sense. ....except for when the Angels go boom and flood the world in rich gooey blood and gore.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Mar 03, 2009 8:07 pm Or, y'know...the titties.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Mar 03, 2009 8:11 pm Oh yeah....I forgot the titties.... and you almost get a full on close up and see what Kaworu's packing (or not depending on what you believe) in the final scene.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Mar 03, 2009 8:42 pm maybe the ratings will be "incremental" 1.0 rated PG, 2.0 rated PG-13, 3.0/4.0 rated R, makes sense IMO

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 03, 2009 8:58 pm I just wanted to mention in IRC today some members were wondering if maybe this showing would cut things out. While that's a possibility, personally I think that maybe 1.0 would be left as is and simply given the "PG" rating since it's felt it's no worse then an anime with that rating? I mean, keep in mind that Death and Rebirth was given a PG-13 rating in the states (IIRC) while EoE was given an R rating. Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Mar 03, 2009 9:56 pm I don't recall there being any MPAA rating ever assigned to an Eva media product, I think they're all effectively unrated of course I could be wrong, though

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Mar 03, 2009 11:39 pm 1.0 isn't rated PG. It's not rated at all, in America or in Japan. The PG rating was probably just someone's estimate of what its rating would be, as it certainly hasn't been officially rated by the MPAA.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Wed Mar 04, 2009 12:59 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: maybe the ratings will be "incremental" 1.0 rated PG, 2.0 rated PG-13, 3.0/4.0 rated R, makes sense IMO

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Mar 04, 2009 1:53 am well instrumentality is like future sex...

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Mar 04, 2009 2:04 am NAveryW wrote: 1.0 isn't rated PG. It's not rated at all, in America or in Japan. The PG rating was probably just someone's estimate of what its rating would be, as it certainly hasn't been officially rated by the MPAA.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 04, 2009 9:21 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: ...keep in mind that Death and Rebirth was given a PG-13 rating in the states (IIRC) while EoE was given an R rating. Just a thought.

[]:             Quote Post by JeffersonLeeEng » Wed Mar 04, 2009 9:26 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Anyone up for a meetup?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 04, 2009 9:34 am JeffersonLeeEng wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Anyone up for a meetup?

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Mar 05, 2009 1:55 pm This is a good news for me since I don't live too far from DC and it the cloest thing coming to Maryland since I doubt their will be a screening of the movie in Maryland. I will buy tickets when I get home !

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Mar 05, 2009 3:55 pm Darn. DC is six hours away. There's no chance of a Hartford showing, now is there?

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu Mar 05, 2009 10:04 pm DC is only three hours for me. I hope tickets are not sold out by next weekend.

[]:             Quote Post by bryan2369 » Sat Mar 07, 2009 12:22 am I'm definitely going. I live in Silver Spring and it's just a metro ride away for me. Might even bike if the weather's good.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Mar 10, 2009 6:09 pm I already got my tickets. Anyone else going to be their on the 28th?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Mar 10, 2009 6:15 pm Wish I could. DC is but a 100 minute drive away but alas I have a film shoot set up for that day and I can't miss it. [/quote]
